rules.name: run-mise-all-before-commit
rules.evidence: ALWAYS run `mise run all` before committing any changes. This runs code quality checks and formatting that must pass for CI to succeed. Never skip this step

rules.name: version-bump-requires-all-files-updated
rules.evidence: When updating the version number for a new release, you must update ALL of these files: 1. lua/claudecode/init.lua ... 2. scripts/claude_interactive.sh ... 3. scripts/lib_claude.sh ... 4. CHANGELOG.md

rules.name: new-integration-requires-fixture
rules.evidence: Create Fixture Configuration: **REQUIRED** - Add a complete Neovim config in `fixtures/[integration-name]/`

rules.name: new-mcp-tool-write-format-and-tests
rules.evidence: Implement Handler: Return MCP format: {content: [{type: "text", text: JSON}]} ... Create Tests: Both unit tests and integration tests required

rules.name: new-mcp-tool-edit-format-and-tests
rules.evidence: MCP Compliance: All tools must return proper MCP format with JSON-stringified content ... VS Code Compatibility: New tools must match VS Code extension behavior exactly

uncompilable: "Best Practice: Always use mise run all at the end of editing sessions" — general workflow advice already covered by the concrete pre-commit trigger, no separate action to bind
Security Considerations bullets (local-only WebSocket, 128-bit token format) — descriptive facts about the system, not instructions about an action
closeAllDiffTabs scope description ("never touches diffs created by other tools") — describes existing behavior, not an instruction to follow
Logging Best Practices (choose DEBUG/WARN/ERROR by event type) — depends on semantic judgment about the event, not a syntactic pattern a trigger can match
Test Organization Principles (Isolation/Mocking/Coverage/Performance/Clarity) — general testing style guidance, not tied to a specific action
Terminal diff_opts example note "external_terminal_cmd — Required for external provider" — documentation for end users' own config, not a rule about editing this repo
"Update CLAUDE.md for architectural changes, PROTOCOL.md for protocol changes" — trigger ("architectural change") too vague to bind to a tool/pattern
Recommended Development Flow steps 1-5 — general procedural workflow description, not tied to a specific tool call
